title: NeRF Volume Rendering
learning_objective: Explain how NeRF maps 5D coordinates to density/color and integrates samples along camera rays to synthesize a pixel or view.
source_excerpt: NeRF represents a scene as a fully connected network mapping spatial location and viewing direction to volume density and view-dependent emitted radiance. Views are synthesized by querying 5D coordinates along camera rays and using volume rendering to project colors and densities into an image.

symbol_ledger.visual_role: Field module queried by sample points.
Color contribution attached to a sample.

symbol_ledger.meaning: Accumulated transmittance up to depth t.

causal_steps.plain_language: A camera pixel corresponds to a ray passing through the 3D scene volume.
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: A yellow ray leaves the camera and cuts through a translucent volume.

causal_steps.plain_language: The renderer samples positions along that ray.
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: Evenly spaced blue beads appear along the ray.

causal_steps.plain_language: Each sample and viewing direction are sent into the neural field.
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: Each bead sends a query into a green field module and receives color plus density.

causal_steps.plain_language: Colors are accumulated along the ray, weighted by density and transmittance.
causal_steps.formula_link: C(r) = integral T(t) sigma(r(t)) c(r(t), d) dt
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: Color chips stack into the final pixel while the transmittance ribbon fades with depth.

misconceptions.risk: Viewers may think NeRF stores an explicit voxel grid.
misconceptions.animation_guardrail: Show a continuous field module queried at points rather than a fixed voxel table.

misconceptions.risk: Viewers may think every sampled point contributes equally.
misconceptions.animation_guardrail: Use density and transmittance to modulate contribution opacity.

misconceptions.risk: Viewers may treat the single-ray example as the whole image.
misconceptions.animation_guardrail: End by sweeping many rays across the image plane.
